providerExtensions.name: Predefined agent prompt as hook action
providerExtensions.description: Hook actions can be a predefined agent prompt (not a shell command). When the trigger fires, Kiro runs the specified prompt through its agent instead of executing a shell command. This is in addition to standard shell command actions.

providerExtensions.name: Spec task lifecycle triggers (before/after spec task execution)
providerExtensions.description: Hooks can fire before and after spec task execution — Kiro-specific triggers tied to its spec/planning workflow (spec tasks are a distinct Kiro concept). Not related to standard file or tool events.

providerExtensions.name: Agent turn completion trigger
providerExtensions.description: A hook trigger that fires after each agent turn completes. Allows automated follow-up actions after every agent response in a session.

providerExtensions.name: Hooks configured via IDE UI only
providerExtensions.description: Kiro hooks ('Agent hooks') are created and managed exclusively through the IDE panel or by asking Kiro in natural language ('Ask Kiro to create a hook'). No documented config file format — hooks are IDE-panel state, not portable YAML or JSON files.
